* Documentation \- adherence to standards
* Broad general Logistics and Supply Chain knowledge
* Continuous improvement mindset
* Process design and documentation skills
* Business requirement analysis skills

title: BUSINESS ANALYST (INDUSTRIAL ENGINEER)
location: Cape Town, WC, ZA
description:
**Listing reference:** cartr\_000815
**Listing status:** Online
**Apply by:** 31 July 2026
**Position summary**


**Industry:** IT \& Internet
**Job category:** Others: Transport and Logistics
**Location:** Cape Town
**Contract:** Permanent
**EE position:** No
**Introduction**


Picup, a last\-mile delivery and technology\-focused company, powered by a scalable crowd\-sourced driver network, seeks an analytical Industrial Engineer who combines process optimization expertise with data analysis capabilities to drive improvements in our last\-mile delivery operations. This role bridges operational excellence with data\-driven decision making by applying industrial engineering to last\-mile delivery.
**Job description**


**Key Responsibilities:**

**Process Analysis \& Optimization:**

* Apply industrial engineering principles to analyse and optimize delivery operations.
* Use insights and results derived from historical delivery data to optimize future operations.
* Incorporate geospatial and operational simulation results into recommendations.
* Identify correlations between different operational variables and performance outcomes.
* Monitor and analyse KPIs related to delivery performance, fleet utilization, and operational costs.
* Develop deep operational knowledge of our delivery platforms and systems to inform improvement recommendations.
* Design and maintain methodology of a Client Prioritisation Scoring Matrix system.
* Directly contribute to initiatives in modelling feasibility, risks, impact and suggest recommendations.
* Stay current on last\-mile delivery and technology trends to benchmark and inform strategies.
* Investigate internal processes to identify areas of waste and ways of improving.

 **Data Analysis \& Insights:**

* Collaborate with Data and technology stakeholders to gather operational data.
* Write SQL queries to extract operational and spatial data for analysis.
* Perform exploratory data analysis to identify patterns and trends in delivery performance.
* Track and evaluate ROI (Return On Investment) of initiatives and new processes.
* Generate reports for process\-level performance, KPI’s, SLA’s and success metrics.

 **Stakeholder Management \& Communication:**

* Prepare and present analytical findings and recommendations to leadership and other stakeholders.
* Collaborate with operations teams to implement process improvements.
* Collaborate with the Data and Data Engineering stakeholders to ensure data accessibility.
* Prepare and maintain business proposals, process documentation, and analytical methodologies.
* Manage multiple projects simultaneously while meeting deadlines.
* Communicate complex analytical findings to non\-technical stakeholders.

**Technical Implementation:**

* Develop process simulation models, cost\-benefit models and capacity models.
* Create and maintain process flow diagrams, model documentation, and documentation for existing and proposed workflows.
* Support the integration of analytical findings into operational procedures.
* Ensure compliance with data privacy regulations, logistics standards and safety protocols.

 **Ad Hoc:**

* Support capacity planning and resource allocation initiatives.
* Ad hoc analysis, queries, and business support

**Minimum requirements**


**Required Qualifications:**

* Bachelor's degree in Industrial Engineering
* 5\-7 years of experience in process improvement or operations analysis.
* A big plus: Experience is combination of Supply Chain / Logistics and Startup Technology related businesses

 **Competencies:**

* Strong analytical and structured problem\-solving skills
* Proven process and systems modelling experience
* Excellent time management and prioritization skills
* Proficiency in data extraction and analysis using Microsoft Excel / Google Sheets and SQL
* Extensive experience with process mapping and optimization
* Basic understanding of statistical analysis principles
* Visualise complex information using graphs and other visualisations
* Good understanding of Programming and Software Engineering concepts
* Excellent client engagement and expectation management skills

 **Technical Skills:**

* Process mapping and optimization tools e.g. Visio, Lucidchart \- intermediate to advanced
* Microsoft Excel / Google Sheets \- intermediate to advanced
* SQL (Structured Query Language) \- basic to intermediate
* Data visualization tools e.g. Looker Studio, Tableau, Power BI \- basic
* A Plus: GIS (Geographic Information System) experience e.g. ArcGIS, QGIS \- basic

title: Manager, Grade 1: Spatial Analyst
location: Cape Town, WC, ZA
description:
**Manager, Grade 1: Spatial Analyst**

**Salary Level 11**

**Fixed\-Term Contract of Employment for five (5\) years**

**All\-inclusive remuneration package of R 932 292\.00 per annum**

**Kirstenbosch National Botanical Garden, Cape Town**

**Biodiversity Information \& Policy Advice**

**Reference Number: Eco\-DRR/2026/0010**

  

The South African National Biodiversity Institute (SANBI) is looking for a self\-motivated and professional individual to oversee and provide advanced spatial analysis and GIS technical support across the three components of the Eco\-DRR project, ensuring the delivery of high\-quality geospatial outputs that inform planning, reporting, and decision\-making.


This position requires a degree in Environmental Science, Geoinformatics, Natural Sciences, Geography, Ecology, or equivalent qualification at NQF 7\. A relevant post\-graduate qualification at NQF 8 will be an added advantage. This position also requires 8 years of work experience in the relevant field, 4 years of which should be in spatial analysis or first line of management of GIS projects.  



**The following competencies are required for the candidate to be successful in this position:**  

  

Advanced knowledge and demonstrated experience in GIS, spatial analysis including data collection, management and flow, and environmental spatial planning. Extensive experience in leading and managing spatial projects. Proven experience in the development and application of spatial decision\-support tools, vulnerability and risk mapping, landscape and ecosystem assessments, and spatial prioritization approaches. Demonstrated ability to interpret and translate complex spatial information into accessible technical products, maps, reports, and science communication outputs to support planning, decision\-making, monitoring, evaluation, learning, and reporting processes. Strong leadership and ability to supervise, mentor, and build the technical capacity of employees and project partners in spatial planning and GIS applications. Expected to have advanced knowledge of compliance and risk management techniques and effective people, budget and financial management skills. South African Council for Natural Scientific Profession (SACNASP) or Plato registration will be advantageous and the candidate must be in possession of a valid driver’s licence with one year practical driving experience.

 **Key Performance Responsibilities include:**

title: Integration Engineer/Developer
location: Cape Town, WC, ZA
description:
**Why Join Cape Union Mart?**


Cape Union Mart is South Africa’s leading outdoor, fashion, and lifestyle retailer, with a proud heritage of delivering exceptional quality and service for over 90 years. Our IT Division is at the forefront of digital transformation, driving innovation and efficiency across all business systems. As part of our dynamic and collaborative IT team, you will play a crucial role in shaping the future of our enterprise solutions, working on cutting\-edge technology, and contributing to impactful projects in a fast\-paced retail environment. If you’re passionate about technology, problem\-solving, and making a difference, Cape Union Mart is the perfect place to grow your career!

**Job Purpose:**


The Integration Engineer will be responsible for designing, configuring, and maintaining Oracle Integration Cloud (OIC) solutions that enable seamless integration between third\-party systems and Oracle Cloud. Additionally, the candidate will play a key role in developing and maintaining Oracle APEX applications, ensuring high\-performance, scalable, and secure web\-based solutions that support business needs. The role requires strong technical expertise in OIC, API integrations, middleware technologies, and Oracle APEX development. The role includes out\-of\-hours support for critical systems as part of a standby rotation.

**Key Responsibilities:**

* Develop and configure Oracle Integration Cloud (OIC) solutions to handle transformations and orchestrations between third\-party systems and Oracle Cloud.
* Design, develop, and maintain Oracle APEX applications, ensuring they align with business requirements and deliver a seamless user experience.
* Work with product owners and technical leads to understand integration and application development requirements, providing scalable solutions.
* Ensure timely delivery of assigned development tasks while adhering to quality standards and deadlines.
* Collaborate with QA teams, analysts, and other stakeholders to analyze requirements, develop user stories, and manage backlog items.
* Provide technical expertise in system integrations, API development, and middleware solutions.
* Monitor, troubleshoot, and optimize integration solutions to enhance performance and scalability.
* Participate in Agile development processes, including standup meetings, sprint planning, and reviews.
* Build prototypes and proof\-of\-concepts (POCs) to explore new integration and application solutions.

**Required Technical and Professional Expertise:**

**Experience:**

* Minimum 2 years of relevant experience in Oracle Integration Cloud Services (OIC).
* Minimum 2 years of experience in Oracle APEX for web\-based application development.

**Technical Skills:**

* API integrations and REST/SOAP web services.
* Data transformation and orchestration between multiple systems.
* Middleware technologies and best practices.
* Strong SQL and PL/SQL programming expertise.
* Other programming language eg: C\# would be advantageous.
* Experience in developing interactive, responsive, and user\-friendly web applications using Oracle APEX.
* Building dynamic reports, charts, dashboards, and forms in APEX.
* JavaScript, HTML, and CSS knowledge for front\-end enhancements.
* Authentication and security best practices in APEX applications.
* Hands\-on experience in integration development, API design, and cloud\-based solutions.
* Experience working with JSON, XML, and SQL databases for integration and APEX applications.
* Familiarity with CI/CD pipelines and DevOps best practices for integration and application deployment.

**Professional Competencies:**

* Ability to work in an agile environment and deliver within tight deadlines.
* Strong analytical and problem\-solving skills.
* Excellent communication skills to collaborate with cross\-functional teams.
* Self\-motivated and capable of working independently while maintaining alignment with team objectives.

**Preferred Qualifications:**

* Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, or a related field.
* Certifications in Oracle Integration Cloud Services (OICS), Oracle APEX Development, or Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI).

**Additional Information:**

* This is a permanent/full\-time position based at Cape Union Mart’s IT Division.
* Occasional travel may be required based on project needs.
* The role requires the ability to work in a fast\-paced retail environment with evolving business requirements.

  

*Please note: The Cape Union Mart Group is committed to transformation. Appointments and promotions will be made based on candidates who best meet the requirements for the position. Candidates that enhance the diversity of the team will be given preference, in line with our Employment Equity plan.*

description:

* Proficient in data visualization tools like Power BI, Tableau, or Looker.
* Knowledge of SQL for querying databases.
* Proven understanding of fundamental statistical analytical techniques (Process Analytical skills such as Six Sigma or Prince 2 would be advantageous)

* Excellent communication and storytelling skills to convey data insights to technical and non\-technical audiences.
* Strong problem\-solving skills and attention to detail.
* Ability to develop practical solutions based on outputs of data
* Familiarity with predictive analytics and machine learning techniques is a bonus.
* Microsoft application proficiency; in Excel and PowerPoint
* Understanding of our client’s systems, processes and best practice

title: FinOps Analyst
location: Cape Town, WC, ZA
description:
### **Who we are**


Yoco was founded in 2015, and we’ve been breaking down barriers, unlocking economic opportunities and opening commerce for people to thrive ever since.


We’re the payments provider for over 200,000 self\-employed, and process over US$3 billion annually.


We have ±350 team members globally, all with a bias for boldness and a passion for simple, progressive solutions. We believe in uniting different people to solve hard problems, together.


Our mission of making society more equal requires a variety of people, with different backgrounds and points of view, to keep building solutions that make life easier for emerging businesses.


We’re growing fast. With growth comes compelling challenges. If you’re an ambitious problem\-solver, with big ideas, a passion for doing good, bright, grounded and courageous; you’re likely to thrive at Yoco.


We don’t stop pushing. We break things to rebuild. We challenge ourselves and each other. We’re constantly evolving \- and we’re doing it fast.


Grow With Us.

### **About the****Cloud Engineering** **team**


The Cloud Engineering team provides the platforms, tooling, and operational foundations that enable Yoco’s engineering teams to build, deploy, and operate reliable services at scale. We manage and evolve our cloud environments across AWS, Azure, and GCP, ensuring our infrastructure remains secure, performant, resilient, and cost\-effective.


As Yoco continues to scale, cloud and SaaS spend have become increasingly important strategic levers. The Cloud Engineering team plays a key role in ensuring that technology investments deliver measurable business value while enabling engineering teams to move quickly and confidently.

### **About the role**


As a FinOps Analyst, you will be responsible for ensuring Yoco derives maximum value from every rand spent across our cloud and SaaS ecosystem.


Reporting to the Cloud Operations team within Cloud Engineering, you will serve as the bridge between Engineering, Finance, and Procurement, bringing visibility, accountability, and optimisation to technology spend across AWS, Azure, GCP, and our core SaaS platforms.


You will own and drive the FinOps lifecycle across Cloud Engineering spend and associated technology platforms by improving cost transparency, identifying optimisation opportunities, governing SaaS utilisation, and partnering with engineering teams to embed cost\-conscious decision\-making into day\-to\-day operations.


This role is ideal for someone with a strong cloud infrastructure background who has developed a passion for cloud economics, cost optimisation, and using data to drive operational and commercial outcomes. Success in this role requires a combination of analytical rigour, technical credibility, commercial awareness, and the ability to influence stakeholders without formal authority.

### **What you will be doing**

* Develop and maintain cost visibility dashboards that provide accurate allocation of cloud and SaaS spend across departments, products, and projects.
* Monitor, analyse, and optimise spend across AWS, Azure, and GCP through effective management of Reserved Instances, Savings Plans, Committed Use Discounts, and other optimisation mechanisms.
* Identify, quantify, and drive cost\-saving opportunities by partnering with engineering teams on initiatives such as rightsizing, storage optimisation, and workload efficiency improvements.
* Govern SaaS utilisation across key platforms, ensuring licensing remains aligned to business needs and eliminating unnecessary spend.
* Build strong relationships with Engineering, Finance, and Procurement stakeholders to establish FinOps best practices and operational processes.
* Lead commercial discussions with technology vendors to negotiate favourable pricing, contract terms, and enterprise agreements.
* Investigate anomalies, proactively detect cost spikes, and provide meaningful recommendations to mitigate financial risk.
* Deliver regular reporting and insights to leadership, enabling informed decision\-making around technology investment and cloud consumption.

### **About you**

* You have 3–8 years of experience in FinOps, Cloud Operations, Cloud Engineering, DevOps, Platform Engineering, or a closely related field.
* You have hands\-on experience managing and optimising cloud spend within AWS and ideally across Azure and GCP.
* You can demonstrate measurable cost optimisation outcomes and clearly articulate the actions taken to achieve them.
* You have a strong understanding of cloud pricing models, discount structures, and optimisation techniques across major cloud providers.
* You are comfortable interrogating large datasets and can independently build analyses using SQL, spreadsheets, billing exports, or other analytical tools.
* You understand cloud infrastructure concepts and can engage credibly with engineers on topics such as compute, storage, networking, and workload optimisation.
* You have experience managing SaaS licensing, utilisation, renewals, and vendor relationships.
* You are comfortable negotiating commercial agreements and working with procurement stakeholders.
* You are highly collaborative and know how to influence priorities across multiple teams without relying on formal authority.
* You enjoy solving complex problems, challenging assumptions, and turning data into actionable business outcomes.

### **Bonus points**

* FinOps Certified Practitioner (FOCP) or FinOps Certified Professional certification.
* Experience operating within FinTech, SaaS, cloud\-native, or high\-growth technology environments.
* Experience managing multi\-million\-rand cloud budgets.
* Experience consolidating SaaS tooling into enterprise\-wide commercial agreements.

### **The people we’re looking for**


We’re looking for people who want to grow and have a thirst for learning. And as Yoco grows, we hope they stay with us for the long\-term.


Building solutions for a more equal society is a daunting task \- and it’s not for everyone. We never stop pushing, we break things to rebuild, and we challenge ourselves and our teammates. We start over, we constantly evolve \- and we do it fast. We know that it’s just the right kind of meaningful madness for our kind of visionary human.


So, who are you? You’re someone who resonates with our mission and our values, and you’re relentlessly effective in your execution.


You’re a curious problem\-solver with a passion for doing good. You’re bright, grounded, experimental and bold. You play open cards and get stuck in. You’re not afraid of change. You close the loop.


At Yoco, we laugh, embrace each other’s quirks, and support one another’s growth, all while staying authentic.


If this sounds like your kind of challenge, apply below and come grow with us.

*We encourage applicants from diverse backgrounds to apply and ask that you please send your application in English and help us reduce unconscious bias by leaving out your picture, age, address, and other unnecessary information in your CV.*

title: Databricks Data Engineer
location: Cape Town, WC, ZA
description:
**Position: Data Engineer \- Intermediate**  

  

**(Hybrid/Pinelands, Cape Town)**


Confluent Services, South Africa’s largest and multi\-award winning debt management company, has a position available for a Data Engineer.


This individual will join the Data Team and will be accountable for data engineering design, data ingestion and support across on\-premise and cloud\-based platforms.


**What’s in it for you?**

Being South Africa’s largest company of its kind, Confluent is continuously growing, with no signs of stopping. At Confluent, **we build on current\-generation platforms like Databricks and Microsoft Fabric,** giving you hands\-on exposure to tools and patterns that are shaping the future of data engineering. This is not a legacy maintenance role, **you’ll be involved in designing, building, and evolving data solutions from the ground up,** with real room for creativity and engineering judgement.


You’ll work alongside a team of high caliber senior data engineers, where knowledge sharing is part of how we operate. Technical discussions are encouraged, opinions are respected, and decisions are driven by merit rather than hierarchy. Our team is actively involved in AI and machine learning initiatives, giving you exposure beyond traditional data pipelines.

**We value clean architecture, thoughtful design, and creativity.** You will have the opportunity to influence how things are built, contribute to architectural decisions, and help shape the evolution of our data platform.


Culturally, we are a diverse, respectful, and collaborative team. We invest in people, support continuous learning, and create an environment where engineers can grow both technically and professionally. Whether you want to deepen your engineering expertise or broaden your impact across the business, you’ll find room to do so here.


Beyond the work itself, Confluent offers a stable, growing environment where the data you build helps real South Africans improve their financial outcomes, giving purpose and impact to the systems you design.

**Other key benefits**:


* We have a vibrant office in a lively part of the city, meaning you have easy access to stores and outlets if you need.
* We offer company benefits – medical aid, retirement annuity, Bursaries and Insurance
* We have a culture of growth, meaning you have access to move both upwards or across in the business if you want to.

**Requirements**

**Candidates must be able to demonstrate:**

* **3 years experience as a data engineer or in a similar role,** designing and implementing data pipelines, data integration and ETL processes. Practical experience in Azure, Azure Data Factory and Azure Databricks is preferred.
* Proven experience in Python/PySpark and SQL/SparkSQL programming skills.
* At least 3 years of experience in Information Technology – specifically in technical data roles e.g. data engineering, data analytics or data science.
* Knowledge of data management and storage solutions, including SQL Server Database, Azure Data Lake Storage or similar.
* Having good communication skills and being able to operate in a team environment.
* Excellent attention to detail and problem\-solving skills related to data, data structures and algorithms.

**Key responsibilities will include:**

* Contribute to the architecture and design of the Confluent Data Platform and ingestion patterns.
* Implement and support the scalable data solutions on the Microsoft Azure cloud platform, including Azure Databricks.
* Develop, maintain and optimise robust production data pipelines for data ingestion, processing, and transformation to support analytics, machine learning and other downstream data\-driven services.
	+ Ensure data security, quality and integrity across all of the pipelines and related data processes.
* Collaborate with the data product owner, analysts, data scientists, and business stakeholders to understand data requirements and implement effective data workflows.
* Assist in data initiatives and support technical teams including architects, analysts, software developers, and data scientists.

**Qualifications:**

* Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field.
* Certifications in Azure, Microsoft Fabric Data Engineering, Azure Databricks Data Engineering or similar or equivalent practical experience.

**Company Description** **ABOUT AVERY DENNISON CORPORATION**


Avery Dennison (NYSE:AVY) is a global leader in labeling and packaging materials and solutions. With operations in more than 50 countries and more than 25,000 employees worldwide, Avery Dennison serves customers with insights and innovations that help make brands more inspiring and the world more intelligent.


Headquarters in Glendale, California**. Learn more at** **www.averydennison.com****.**

Overall Commercial and FP\&A support to Middle East/Africa (MEA) Business and Region. Actively support the Commercial and Sales teams with commercial guidance. Custodian of BI development and related data integrity/maintenance.

* Assume responsibility for all sales and commercial reporting
* Review master data integrity (BI driver)

**COMMERCIAL BUSINESS PARTNERING: (35%)**

* Proactively review SKU profitability and discussions with Sales team
* Finance and Commercial related support to the Sales Teams
* Flag risk areas of low margins, pricing anomalies, etc
* Provide proactive commercial insights and feedback to Management
* Identify, interpret and advise on the impact of costs changes

**GENERAL: (10%)**

* Training support to the business on areas of expertise i.e. Google, BI etc.
* General finance support to the business

title: WIM Investigator II, AmSec Workplace Incident Management
location: Cape Town, WC, ZA
description:
**DESCRIPTION**
---------------


The Workplace Incident Management (WIM) Team is searching for a WIM Investigator to join the team. In this role, you will oversee incident response for the Europe, Middle East and Africa (EMEA) Region. This position is an individual contributor and will report to the Regional WIM Manager, EMEA Region.
  

  

The WIM Investigator assess and manages workplace incidents involving persons exhibiting inappropriate, concerning, or threatening behavior. To accomplish this, the WIM Investigator will work with multi\-disciplinary teams comprised of cross\-functional stakeholders (e.g., Human Resources, Employee Relations, Legal), and security partners (e.g., site security, intelligence analysts) to assess the behavior, investigate actions, and implement the appropriate protective measures to resolve the concern while appropriately supporting persons involved.  

  

To be successful in this role, you must have experience in Behavioral Threat Assessment and Management (BTAM), Investigations, Incident Management, Security Operations, Social Work or Law Enforcement. The ideal candidate must have a passion for helping people; has experience managing multiple caseloads of varying priority; possess strong analytical skills; demonstrates high judgment during high stress situations; must be an excellent oral and written communicator; and have a high degree of integrity.  

  

Key job responsibilities  

* Build and maintain collaborative partnerships with cross\-functional stakeholders (e.g., Human Resources, Employee Relations, Legal), and security partners (site security, intelligence analysts).
* Conduct comprehensive threat assessments or investigations into workplace incidents involving internal or external persons exhibiting inappropriate, abusive, or threatening behavior.
* Organize, prioritize and investigate multiple incidents concurrently, as part of a caseload, with overlapping deadlines and changing priorities.
* Conduct interviews of impacted employees, witnesses and/or persons of interest utilizing interviewing techniques.
* Analyze CCTV video, access control systems and other data collection capabilities, in response to incidents in order to identify behaviors, root causes, locations, potential evidence, and/or person(s) involved.
* Use Microsoft and Adobe products to include a cloud\-based case management system for documentation of threat assessment or investigation.
* Deliver detailed, well\-organized, factual and timely investigative information, reports, assessments, and presentations for internal customers and stakeholders related to workplace incidents.
* Document, report, and appropriately escalate risks, threats or issues to internal or external stakeholders and law enforcement agencies, as required by applicable laws.
**BASIC QUALIFICATIONS**
------------------------

* Experience in Incident Management, Threat Management, Corporate Investigations, Law Enforcement, Security Operations, Crisis Management or a related field that involves conducting threat assessments, workplace investigations, and/or incident response

**PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS**
----------------------------

* Bachelor's degree or equivalent
* Experience in facility/corporate security management, law enforcement, risk management, legal, or related industries

  

Amazon is an equal opportunities employer, and we value your passion to discover, invent, simplify and build. We welcome applications from all members of society irrespective of age, sex, disability, sexual orientation, race, religion or belief. Amazon is strongly committed to diversity within its community and especially welcomes applications from South African citizens who are members of designated groups who may contribute to Employment Equity within the workplace and the further diversification of ideas. In this regard, the relevant laws and principles associated with Employment Equity will be considered when appointing potential candidates. We are required by law to verify your ability to work lawfully in South Africa. Amazon requires that you submit a copy of either your identity document or your passport and any applicable work permit if you are a foreign national, along with an updated curriculum vitae.  

  

Our inclusive culture empowers Amazonians to deliver the best results for our customers. If you have a disability and need a workplace accommodation or adjustment during the application and hiring process, including support for the interview or onboarding process, please visit https://amazon.jobs/content/en/how\-we\-hire/accommodations for more information. If the country/region you’re applying in isn’t listed, please contact your Recruiting Partner.

title: Supply Chain Analyst
location: Stellenbosch, WC, ZA
description:
* Purpose of the job



\|


Be a first\-class business partner for the James Sedgwick Whisky Distillery, Van Ryn distillery and JVs by generating and collating financial data, ensure integrity of financial data. Identify and access performance and risks and take or recommend business decisions and actions for the distillery and enable fact\-based decision making and product and operating cost management.



Be the guardian of HEINEKEN assets and reputation through improved financial controls, managed financial risks and clear \& transparent information on business performance.


* Key responsibilities



\|


* Be a co\-pilot to Sites and JVs Production teams in decision making through solicited and unsolicited advice based on financial analysis and in\-depth knowledge of business operations.
* Support the business planning cycle of the Site (Strategic Plan, Annual Plan and Latest Estimates).
* Prepare and critically analyse monthly results and deliver timely management reports which include commentaries, KPIs, graphs, internal/external insights and/or benchmarks.
* Manage and assess business unit performance against standards including: OEE, O/T, costing standards, Routing on SAP and recommend performance improvement or standards adjustment
* Analyse and report on weekly and monthly production order variances, provide early warnings on deviations, advise on corrective/mitigating actions; review loss trends; ensure that the production Bill of Materials are correct and up to date.
* Ensure reliability \& integrity of accounting records (accounts and cost centres) and financial information (P\&L; and balance sheet) through regular controls and financial awareness of functions.
* Ensure short\-term interval controls are effective. Continuously review sites and JVs financial control processes and identify possible risks. Propose and implement solutions/controls to strengthen control environment and foster a culture of continuous improvement.
* Perform monthly audits to ensure adherence to internal control principles and assist in stock verification as and when required.
* Support business unit to ensure adherence to financial control policies (financial, IT and materials) and procedures and good corporate governance principles \& practices.
* Adhere to Excise policies and procedures and ensure documentation and administration is suitably maintained.
* Ensure adherence and provide necessary support to the capital expenditure process as well as ensuring validity and accuracy of the asset register.
* Involved in stock management and assist with stock takes, identification of slow moving and obsolete stock. Escalate to management team any stock risks.
* Participate in cross site audits and stock takes.
* Develop level 2 \& 3 management financial competencies / capability in business unit.
* Implement new ideas and improve methods, systems and work processes that will lead to higher quality, better results, and reduced costs.
* Support and assist site managers in compiling OPEX and CAPEX budgets and forecasts.
* Co\-ordinate the processing of budgets and forecasts
* Managing of the Monthly Barrel reconciliations across sites.
* Responsible for the accurate and timeous end to end processing and reconciliation of all financial transactions, management accounts and reporting relating to the JV’s.


* Job requirements


* + Education
* BCom Accounting or equivalent qualification completed.


* CIMA/ BCom Accounting honours advantages.


* + Experience
* Minimum of 3 years relevant experience in the area of Management Accounting and/or Controlling, ideally in Supply Chain. FMCG experience advantageous.
* Intellectual curiosity and ability to search out facts without prior process/guidance.
* Passion for delivering result, take ownership and translating insights into concise practical plans.
* Strong interpersonal/communication/influencing skills to challenge and win support and drive decision making.
* Excellent team player with strong oral and written communication skills.
* High level of cross functional cooperation: effectively partnering with various teams.
* Ability to work independently and under pressure.
* Drive for improvements and changes.
* Sound knowledge of MS office and SAP with specific importance to manufacturing.
* Ability to prepare simple/medium complexity business cases and models.

title: Junior Credit Risk Analyst
location: Cape Town, WC, ZA
description:
Weaver FinTech Group is a leading digital financial services ecosystem connecting consumers to smarter, more flexible ways to pay, borrow, and protect what matters most. Our brands — Pay Just Now and FinChoice — empower millions of South Africans through innovative Payments, Lending, and Insurance solutions.


We’re a data\-first organisation built on collaboration, innovation, and purpose: to make digital finance simpler, more inclusive, and more human.  

  

**Junior Credit Risk Analyst**  

PayJustNow is a Weaver Fintech company and South Africa's leading Buy Now, Pay Later platform, part of the same group as FinChoice. We let consumers split purchases into interest\-free instalments at checkout across thousands of merchants, while giving retailers a powerful tool to grow sales. We are an innovative, consumer\-facing fintech obsessed with customer experience, innovation and building great tech in pursuit of our vision to be our customers' favourite way to pay.  

Our team consists of passionate individuals that work in multi\-disciplinary teams, where our strength in execution lies in our teamwork. Collectively we have driven significant customer growth and profitability, and we focus on the detail, being hands\-on and executing against our plans – a scale\-up that thinks and executes like a start\-up.  

If you're looking for a place where you will positively impact lives, learn more about and contribute to the Fintech industry, and be involved in multiple products, projects and technologies, then look no further than PayJustNow.

 **Role Overview**  

As the Junior Credit Risk Analyst, you will help the business get more value out of every data source it touches. Working closely with senior analysts and the Credit Risk Executive, you will gather, clean and combine data from across the group, the credit bureaux and alternative data sources, and turn it into analysis that informs strategic credit risk decisions. This is a hands\-on, learning\-rich role for an early\-career analyst who is curious about data and wants to see their analysis drive real decisions across underwriting, limits, collections and portfolio monitoring.

**More about the role**

* Source, extract and prepare data from across the group, credit bureau feeds and alternative data sources, building clean, reliable datasets for analysis
* Help identify and unlock new value from existing and emerging data sources – spotting useful signals, new attributes and data quality issues
* Use SQL/Python to obtain data from the Data Warehouse and support the maintenance of analytical data assets
* Perform 'what if' and ad\-hoc analysis to support credit strategy across acquisition, limits, collections and recoveries
* Support the monitoring of risk\-based strategies in acquisition to ensure only customers with the correct risk levels are acquired
* Help review credit limits to ensure the business exposure strategy is met
* Build and maintain reporting and dashboards that track credit quality of business booked and the credit portfolio
* Support the development and back\-testing of scorecards and predictive models
* Use a data\-oriented approach to work with others in solving business problems around profitability, marketing, risk and operations
* Assist the Credit Risk Executive and senior analysts on key projects, ensuring strong planning and communication throughout

**More about you**

**Experience \& Skills**

* A minimum of 1–2 years' experience in Credit Risk, data analytics or a related field (recent graduates with strong analytical internships will be considered)
* A numerate degree e.g. BCom, B Bus Sci, B Eng, BSc
* Good computer literacy skills with proficiency in MS Excel
* The ability to creatively solve problems
* The ability to interrogate and interpret numerical and statistical information

**Advantages**

* SQL \& Snowflake
* Exposure to credit bureau and/or alternative data sources
* Python/R or equivalent
* Retail credit or BNPL experience
* Use of Gen AI

**Attributes \& Behaviours**

* Natural curiosity around data and problem solving
* Strong analytical skills and a self\-motivated driver of performance
* Proactive and able to identify, highlight, analyse and action based on information available
* Able to work with other areas to ensure analytical outputs can be driven through to execution to create value
* Excellent interpersonal communication (verbal \& written), assertiveness and judgment skills
* The ability to simply explain complex solutions
* Strong planning, organising and coordinating skills
* Exceptional attention to detail with a thorough approach to work
* A strong can\-do attitude and an energetic positive approach
* A solid work ethic and a passion for digital Financial Services
* Eager to learn, coachable and entrepreneurial by nature

**More about us**


Reciprocity and trust are the foundation of how we work. We believe that each of us show up every day to do our best work and help one another and our customers. Strong, meaningful relationships underpin our culture and success, and we value authenticity and transparency. We value equity and strive to be fair and balanced. We celebrate our differences and understand how they contribute positively to the team.

* We offer a flexible working arrangement and hybrid working
* We support your professional development through coaching, training and bursaries
* We offer employee wellness and support through our ChoiceCare employee assistance programme
* We love celebrating your milestones with you

title: CRM & Data Analyst

description:

title: Yield Analyst
location: Cape Town, WC, ZA
description:
**Le Boat, part of the Travelopia group**, is the world’s leading provider of self\-drive boating holidays, with over 55 years of experience. We offer an unforgettable holiday experience across Europe and Canada's inland waterways. Our fleet, crafted to suit all party sizes and budgets, requires no license or prior boating experience, making it accessible to everyone. With guests from around the globe, including North America, Germany, and France, Le Boat operates in 9 countries with France's Canal du Midi as the most famous and popular cruising ground.

We are looking to hire a **Yield Analyst** to join our team on a **12\-month fixed\-term contract**. Reporting to the Revenue Management Director, you will support pricing decisions across our fleet and destinations. You will analyse sales, booking pace, utilisation, and competitor pricing, recommending and implementing pricing, discounting, and product actions to help achieve revenue and occupancy targets.


Pricing is primarily managed in Excel, supported by Power BI dashboards and Neptune (our reservations system). Over the next 12 months, we are expanding the use of AI within our revenue management workflow, using tools such as Claude, Copilot, and Python\-based models to automate analysis, improve forecasting, and identify pricing opportunities more quickly. You will play a key role in this transition and act as the go\-to person for pricing\-related queries across Commercial, Marketing, Fleet, and MI.


### **What we’ll offer:**


* Competitive base salary of R300,000\-400,000 per annum
* Flexible hybrid working (2 days per week in our Cape Town office)
* Generous paid time off, plus a day off to celebrate your birthday
* Group\-wide travel discounts
* A small, high\-impact team where your work is visible to commercial leadership from day one

### **What you’ll do:**


**Revenue Management**


* Analyse sales, booking pace, utilisation and forecasts, implementing pricing and promotional actions to achieve revenue targets.
* Monitor competitor pricing across key source markets and maintain pricing and discount structures in Neptune and Excel.
* Evaluate performance by boat type, base and source market, recommending corrective actions where required.
* Assess the effectiveness of promotions and discounting activity on revenue and margin.
* Provide pricing support to Marketing and respond to internal commercial queries.


**Forecasting \& Analysis**


* Build and maintain demand forecasts using booking pace, historical trends and booking curves.
* Identify emerging performance trends and recommend actions to improve revenue outcomes.
* Support pricing tests and analysis, including elasticity and promotional effectiveness.


**AI \& Tooling**


* Use AI tools (e.g. Claude, Copilot, ChatGPT) to support analysis, forecasting, reporting and communication.
* Help identify, test and implement AI\-driven improvements to pricing, forecasting and reporting processes.
* Support the development and rollout of new pricing, forecasting and automation tools.


**Reporting \& Insight**


* Build and maintain reporting in Power BI to track pricing performance, occupancy and margin in near real time.
* Produce a clear weekly pricing pack for the Revenue Management Director and commercial leadership.
* Provide cover for the MI team’s weekly reporting suite during absence.


**Systems**


* Become the expert user of Neptune (reservations system), understanding how pricing rules, durations and availability are configured and identifying improvement opportunities.
* Work with the inventory team on fleet planning inputs, including base availability, maintenance windows, route disruptions and duration rules.

### **What you’ll bring:**


**Essential**


* Strong numerical and analytical skills with exceptional attention to detail.
* Advanced Excel skills — comfortable with complex formulas, pivot tables, Power Query, and large datasets. Excel is the primary tool for this role.
* Experience building reports and dashboards in Power BI.
* Confident using AI tools to support analysis, reporting and problem\-solving.
* Commercially minded, with the ability to balance margin, demand, and customer impact.
* Strong communication skills with the ability to explain pricing decisions to stakeholders.
* Calm under pressure and comfortable working in a fast\-paced, highly seasonal environment.
* Inquisitive and detail\-oriented, with a willingness to challenge assumptions and question data.
* Proactive and able to work independently with minimal direction.


**Desirable**


* Previous experience in pricing, yield or revenue management, ideally in travel, hospitality, airlines or car rental.
* Experience applying AI tools to forecasting, pricing, or revenue problems (beyond basic tool usage).
* Understanding of prompt engineering and how to structure AI inputs for reliable analytical output.
* Python and/or SQL skills for data extraction, manipulation or automation.
* Exposure to demand forecasting, price elasticity or segmentation analysis.
* Familiarity with reservation systems, PMS or revenue management platforms.
* Experience with A/B testing or commercial experimentation frameworks.


*We believe people perform best when they can be their true selves, and diverse teams drive better results. We’re committed to fostering a diverse, equitable, and inclusive environment where everyone can succeed.*


*Travelopia ensures an inclusive workplace for all. If you need accessibility support during the recruitment process, please inform us here: Talent@Travelopia.com*

description:

**What you will do in this role**

* Deliver accurate, objective **quality assessments** and report insights to stakeholders including Quality, Operations, and Leadership.
* Identify trends, improvement opportunities, and key drivers impacting **customer experience and service delivery**

title: Senior Retail Media Insights Analyst
location: Brackenfell, WC, ZA
description:
**Senior Retail Media Insights Analyst**


**Role Purpose**

The Insights function is enabled by large\-scale enterprise data assets, advanced analytics, and applied research capabilities to generate actionable insights that support commercial decision\-making, customer growth, and business performance.


The Senior Retail Media Insights Analyst is responsible for analysing complex datasets, identifying trends and opportunities, and translating findings into clear, actionable insights that inform planning, optimisation, and performance improvement across the business. The role partners closely with internal teams and stakeholders to ensure data\-led insights are understood, applied correctly, and embedded into decision\-making processes.


This role requires strong analytical capability, the ability to connect data to business context, and the discipline to deliver accurate, high\-quality insight outputs at scale.


**Job Description**

**Data Analysis and Insight Generation**


* Collect, clean, analyse and interpret data from defined internal and external sources to generate actionable insights.
* Identify trends, patterns, risks and opportunities through structured analysis and deep dives.
* Perform root cause analysis to address defined business questions or performance challenges.
* Ensure data accuracy, integrity and consistency across all analyses and insight outputs.


**Insight Translation and Storytelling**

* Translate analytical findings into business\-relevant insight narratives and recommendations. Present insights in clear, concise and stakeholder\-appropriate language.
* Create insight packs, presentations and reporting outputs aligned to business objectives.


**Reporting and Performance Measurement**

* Develop standard and ad hoc reports, dashboards and commentary to track performance.
* Apply appropriate metrics, benchmarks and performance indicators to support decision\-making.
* Support the continuous improvement of reporting frameworks and measurement approaches.


**Stakeholder Collaboration**

* Partner with cross\-functional teams to understand insight requirements and priorities.
* Act as a trusted analytics partner by supporting effective use of insights in planning and optimisation.
* Support insight enablement by ensuring outputs are correctly interpreted and applied.
* Continuous Improvement and Learning
* Stay current with industry trends, data methodologies and analytical techniques.
* Contribute to improved analytical practices, tools and processes within the Insights function.


**Role\-Specific Responsibilities: Senior Retail Media Analyst**


* Analyse retail media performance data across campaigns, platforms and channels.
* Generate campaign\-level and post\-campaign insights to inform optimisation, planning and future media strategies.
* Support media performance measurement frameworks, tracking methodologies and tagging standards.
* Translate retail media data into actionable insights for sales teams, media partners and clients.
* Contribute to media planning inputs by aligning insights to audience, category and campaign objectives.
* Stay current with retail media trends, tools and measurement methodologies.


**Qualifications and Experience**

* Degree or Diploma in Business, Marketing, Analytics, or a related field – (essential).
* \+5 years' experience in a similar capacity, buying or category management – (essential). Demonstratable hands\-on analytics experience \- generating actionable insights and doing data deep\-dives \- (essential).
* Experience translating data into digestible insights for media sales and client campaign recommendations and presentations \- (essential)
* Proficiency in MS Office and CRM software (e.g., Salesforce) \- (essential)
* Media analysis, data analytics, or related roles/experience in the advertising or marketing industry\- (desirable).
* Demonstratable ability influencing, establishing and maintaining strong relationships with management, internal partners, and external clients \- (desirable)


**Key Competencies and Work Ethic**

* Retail Media Insights and Reporting – Sound understanding of retail media processes for data collection, processing, analysis to provide accurate reports and/or dashboards that meet the requirements of the target audience. Applies sound knowledge and skills in using data visualization tools, developing dashboards and selecting the most relevant key performance metrics in a manner that will enable insights and decision\-making.
* Analytical thinker and resourcefulness\- Readily comprehend new and relatively complex concepts and information, investigates courses of action to identify the most appropriate solution or develop alternative solutions. Able to analyze retail media insights with a desire to learn more about the trends shaping consumer behavior today, and in the future.
* Customer focused \- obsessed about the customer and delivering a digital enriched customer journey experience.
* Collaborative partnering \- Builds meaningful and sound relationships with suppliers across brands, consumer insights, ecommerce, and advanced analytics. Open, honest and direct and comfortable giving and receiving constructive feedback. Thinks and acts both independently as well as collaboratively.
* Communication skills \- Speaks clearly, audibly and at an appropriate pace and provides credible points of an argument in own area of expertise. Writes clearly and succinctly, using correct spelling and grammar; and provides simple points in a straightforward and factual manner. Strong command of the English language with excellent presentation and story telling skills\- Ability to articulates ideas and concepts in a creative and meaningful way.
* Detailed, organized and quality focused – Has an affinity for detail, structure, and efficiency. Is diligent and vigilantly watches over work processes, tasks, and outputs to ensure accuracy while independently actioning and correcting any quality concerns.
* Critical thinker and independent problem solver.
* Time management, planning and operational efficiency \- able to prioritize a high volume of tasks simultaneously in a fast\-paced unpredictable environment. Someone who can spread themselves across multiple tasks simultaneously by working smartly, efficiently, and effectively. Organized with strong time, delivery, and planning management.
* Adapting and responding to change \- adjusts to a fast paced, changing environment; tolerates ambiguity; adapts interpersonal style to suit different people or situations.
* Staying up to date with global media trends and insights.

title: Digital Data Analyst (Rainmaker Media)
location: Brackenfell, WC, ZA
description:

Powered by Africa's largest retailer, Rainmaker Media is positioned to reach over 80% of SA's shoppers directly through its digital and physical media channels. We have exclusive access to the continent's largest retail audience and are using rich shopper data and advanced analytics to create the continent's first precision retail media service. We're currently building a cutting edge, collaborative, passionate team and seeking the best talent there is to join us in shaping our new culture and delivery ambitions!


**Role Purpose**

To streamline data acquisition through advanced tagging and Mobile Measurement Partner (MMP) management, delivering automated, AI\-enhanced ecommerce insights in collaboration with the broader ShopriteX Data teams.


**Role Description**


1\. Digital Data Collection \& Tagging Infrastructure


* Own the end\-to\-end implementation and management of digital tracking via Google Tag Manager (GTM) and other relevant tagging technologies.
* MMP Management: Maintain and optimise the AppsFlyer setup to ensure accurate mobile app attribution and data collection integrity across all mobile campaigns.

title: Business Process Analyst
location: Cape Town, WC, ZA
description:
We are looking for a collaborative mid\-level **Business Process Analyst** to support our Operations Managers by ensuring our operations teams (CX, Sales, Credit and Finance) have the tools, documented processes and training in place to support and facilitate smooth and seamless internal operations that will give our teams every chance to keep our promises to customers and succeed.


**KEY RESPONSIBILITIES:**


* **Process Documentation:** Working closely with allocated Subject Matter Expert/s to document process changes in a standardized and appropriate manner.
* **Subject Matter Expert Support:** Support Subject Matter Experts with the effective execution and rollout of change projects.
* **Customer servicing:** Contribute towards the creation of documentation, process flows and other items that help drive a high standard of customer service. This ranges from funding application processing, customer service requests and transaction processing.
* **Process tooling and training requirements:** Assist with assessing employee and team tooling and training requirements and support specific recommendations aimed at driving optimal productivity.
* **Operations reporting:** Document and share timely and accurate reports provided by Data Scientists and Analysts with key stakeholders so that they are aware of areas of business non\-performance, business risk and service issues.
* **Process control:** Document, implement and monitor processes that drive operational efficiency and effectiveness (i.e. that deliver on our customer promises, enable a productive workforce and meet business monetisation and cost control goals)
* Ensure training material to support processes is accessible and accurate
* **Project Management** Support and coordination of projects or initiatives required to meet the above responsibilities inclusive of developing businesses cases, defining business requirements, assisting with implementation, setting quality standards and measuring ongoing performance


**COMPETENCIES WE ARE LOOKING FOR**


* **Requirements gathering and specification:** The ability to identify and gather requirements from key stakeholders, map business processes and design process improvements in a clear, actionable format that can be used to communicate with the team and drive operational effectiveness.
* **Initiative implementation and delivery:** The ability to build strong relationships and work cross\-collaboratively with the operations teams to iteratively and quickly deliver business improvements (process, tools, workforce ,etc.) that accomplish pre\-defined goals.
* **Quality Assurance:** The ability to proactively identify, prioritize, and resolve business process quality issues across teams, platforms and vendors.
* **Deriving insights from data:** The ability to use data to generate actionable insights, to leverage those insights to achieve goals and to connect those quantified goals to meaningful outcomes for the business.
* **Voice of employees:** The ability to leverage employee feedback in the form of work observation, surveys, 1:1s and other forms of research to understand employee needs and “ways of working” to remove barriers and create new opportunities for process improvement and effectiveness.
* **Employee UX:** The ability to define requirements and influence UX designs that ensure our in\-house tools and configurable partner platforms are easy to use and drive the most efficient and effective serving of customers.


**THE SKILLS AND EXPERIENCE WE'RE LOOKING FOR**


* Grade 12 or equivalent
* Minimum 2\-3 years mid\-level experience in similar role essential
* Process Documentation creation experience essential
* Experience with developing and implementing standard operating procedures
* Experience with managing stakeholders from various areas of the business
* Experience working with technology platforms
* Experience working within an agile, scrum or lean environment
* Proactive self starter who is not afraid of a challenge
