title: Software Engineering Manager
location: Cape Town, WC, ZA
description:
Location: Cape Town


Salary: 80k\-135k per month\+benefits

**Purpose**



Leads a software engineering team with responsibility for people leadership, technical direction, and delivery execution. Ensures the team delivers high‑quality, maintainable software aligned with business objectives, upholds technical standards, and develops engineering capability to support current and future organisational needs.


**Role Impact**


**Budgetary Responsibility**
  

Moderate – Understands and communicates team‑level costs related to infrastructure, licensing, and staffing. Provides input into budget planning and cost‑optimisation opportunities. Supports expense tracking and communicates cost considerations to stakeholders.  

**Financial Impact**
  

Moderate – Decisions influence departmental budgets, resource allocation, vendor spend, and operational efficiencies. Manages and mitigates financial risks within the team’s scope.  

**Scope**
  

Local – Focused on a defined engineering team and its delivery responsibilities.  

**Team Size**
  

Direct Reports: 5–10  

Indirect Reports: None


**Strategic Alignment**


**Commercial**
  

Ensures delivery of features and products that support business goals and customer needs, while managing team efficiency and cost awareness to maximise value.  

**Operations**
  

Drives operational excellence by establishing technical standards, code quality frameworks, and deployment processes. Ensures clear communication of commitments and progress to stakeholders.  

**Delivery**
  

Owns predictable, high‑quality delivery by setting realistic commitments, coordinating with product and quality teams, and maintaining transparent progress visibility.  

**People**
  

Builds engineering capability through structured coaching, mentorship, performance management, and fostering an inclusive, growth‑oriented team culture.  

**Innovation**
  

Identifies and implements improvements in tooling, automation, and engineering practices to enhance productivity and support adoption of emerging technologies.


**Capabilities Required**


**Education**
  

Bachelor’s Degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or related field.  

Advanced Diploma or equivalent technical qualification acceptable with demonstrated management experience.  

**Experience**
  

6–8 years of progressive software engineering experience, including 2–3 years in a formal leadership or management role.  

Informal leadership experience (technical lead, project lead, senior engineer mentoring) considered with strong evidence of team impact.


**Key Responsibilities**


* Leads and manages team performance, providing coaching, mentorship, and career development while ensuring accountability for delivery commitments.
* Owns delivery outcomes for assigned products/services, managing prioritisation, coordination, and communication with product and technical stakeholders.
* Establishes technical direction and standards, ensuring architectural decisions align with long‑term strategy and balance quality with delivery timelines.
* Translates business priorities into clear technical execution plans in partnership with product management and stakeholders.
* Identifies and manages technical risks, including technical debt, testing gaps, security concerns, and performance issues.
* Coordinates with cross‑functional teams to manage dependencies, resolve blockers, and maintain predictable delivery.
* Monitors delivery performance and quality metrics, providing visibility to leadership and enabling data‑driven decision‑making.
* Drives process, tooling, and quality improvements to enhance productivity and reduce manual effort.
* Participates in recruitment, capability planning, and talent management to ensure appropriate team skills and capacity.
* Collaborates with peer managers and engineering leadership on cross‑functional initiatives and technical standards.
* Owns production responsibility for team services, ensuring operational health, participating in incident response, and prioritising stability.


**Operating Network**


**Internal Stakeholders**


* Head of Software Engineering
* Product Management
* Peer Engineering Managers
* Quality Assurance / Testing
* Architects / Technical Leads
* Finance
* Senior Engineers / Technical Leads
* HR
* Infrastructure / Operations


**External Stakeholders**


* Customers / Account Management (where applicable)
* Third‑party integration partners
* Platform and tooling vendors


**Skills Required**



Leadership; Team Building; Coaching \& Mentoring; Performance Management; Conflict Resolution; People Development;

title: DevOps Engineer
location: Century City, WC, ZA
description:
About GLI

At GLI our software development team is a full\-stack workforce operating in an agile environment. We encourage a culture of empowerment, creative expression, a positive work\-life balance, and flexible working hours. GLI has established its operations across international territories in the iGaming industry and continues to develop its software platform. With the infrastructure now in place, our focus is on continuous improvement \- securing it, simplifying it, and keeping it up to date. As a regulated iGaming business, security, compliance, and uptime are central to everything our infrastructure team does.

If you're a driven individual who enjoys challenging projects and a collaborative environment, and you want to join an exciting, fast\-growing industry, this is the place for you.

More at: www.gli\-sa.co.za

Job Purpose

We are looking for a DevOps Engineer to own how our software is built, shipped, run, and monitored \- working shoulder\-to\-shoulder with our development team to build and maintain the environments they depend on. You will own our CI/CD pipelines, containerised services, observability, and our PostgreSQL high\-availability stack.

You carry strong Linux fundamentals on purpose: you and our Systems Operations Engineer share an operational core so that either of you can keep the lights on when the other is unavailable. You'll work very closely with the developers \- building, shipping, and maintaining development environments and delivery pipelines that move features safely from a developer's machine through to production \- and continuously improving how all of that runs.

Key Responsibility Areas

● Build, maintain, and improve the development, staging, and production environments the development team relies on \- keeping parity between them and making promotion between environments smooth and predictable.

● Build and maintain continuous integration and delivery pipelines (Bitbucket Pipelines) to support development and production deployment.

● Own containerised services \- Docker / Docker Compose, image strategy, and reverse\-proxy/routing (nginx).

● Operate the PostgreSQL high\-availability cluster \- Patroni\-managed failover, etcd consensus/quorum, and HAProxy routing.

● Own observability as a shared platform \- centralised logging (Graylog), service/application monitoring (Zabbix), and alerting (PagerDuty) \- ensuring critical services are continuously monitored.

● Find and remove repetitive or error\-prone tasks through automation.

● Collaborate with developers on infrastructure requirements for new features \- planning, design, documentation, and implementation.

● Manage code flow from local devices through multiple environments to production (the DevOps lifecycle).

● Maintain redundancy and documented, tested backups for the services you own.

● Produce and maintain design, systems, and runbook documentation.

● Continuously optimise existing processes, systems, and infrastructure.

● Participate in an after\-hours and weekend support rotation shared across the infrastructure team.

Required Skills

● Strong Linux system administration (Ubuntu, CentOS/RHEL) \- this is core, not incidental.

● Strong Linux command\-line proficiency.

● Working knowledge of network services (NFS, SMB, LDAP, DNS) and the OSI model.

● Version control proficiency (Git) and package management familiarity (rpm, deb).

Required Experience

● Production Docker / Docker Compose and reverse\-proxy configuration (nginx) with TLS.

● CI/CD pipeline design and operation (Bitbucket Pipelines, or transferable: GitLab CI, GitHub Actions, Jenkins).

● High\-availability PostgreSQL operations \- Patroni, etcd, HAProxy \- including failover and recovery (or strong comparable HA\-database experience).

● Web server technologies (NGINX, Apache HTTPD, HAProxy).

● Centralised logging and monitoring (Graylog/ELK, Zabbix, Prometheus/Grafana).

● Scripting (Bash, Python) and an automation\-first mindset.

● One or more programming languages (Node.js, JavaScript, Java, or PHP) \- enough to work fluently with developers.

● Agile delivery (Jira, Bitbucket) and a change\-control discipline.

Advantageous Experience

● Container orchestration (Kubernetes, OpenShift).

● Infrastructure as code and configuration management (Terraform, Ansible).

● Cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, GCP) \- relevant to our international expansion.

● In\-memory data stores (Redis, Memcached).

● Virtualisation exposure (Proxmox, VMware) \- deepens cover with the Systems Operations Engineer.

● Security audits and standards (PCI\-DSS, ISMS, ISO 27000\), server hardening, and defensive tooling \- important in our regulated industry.

● PKI and certificate lifecycle management (Let's Encrypt, commercial CAs).

Attributes

● Self\-motivated, proactive, and comfortable taking initiative.

● Strong team spirit; collaborates naturally with developers and operations alike.

● Logical and objective, with the creativity to find better ways of working.

● Comfortable under pressure and across multiple concurrent initiatives.

● Clear written and verbal communication with technical and non\-technical staff.

● Flexible and adaptable in day\-to\-day work.

Qualifications

● Degree/Diploma in Information Systems or related field.

● At least 4 years in DevOps and/or Linux operations roles.

● Demonstrated ownership of CI/CD and production runtime systems.

How Vacancies Work

Once we receive your resume, our HR team will review it shortly. If you reach the interview stage, you'll hear from us by email or phone within two weeks. Due to application volumes we're unable to give feedback to every applicant \- but keep an eye out, as new opportunities are posted here regularly.

Pay: R40 000,00 \- R55 000,00 per month

Work Location: Hybrid remote in Century City, Western Cape

title: Systems Operations Engineer
location: Century City, WC, ZA
description:
About GLI

At GLI our software development team is a full\-stack workforce operating in an agile environment. We encourage a culture of empowerment, creative expression, a positive work\-life balance, and flexible working hours. We are looking for an experienced Systems Operations Engineer to own the foundation our platform and our business runs on. In this role you keep our servers, storage, networking, and identity systems up, current, and secure \- and you are the person accountable for the uptime of that infrastructure.

You will work closely with our DevOps Engineer (with deliberately overlapping skills, so the two of you can cover for one another) and alongside our internal IT Systems Administrator. Our infrastructure already spans multiple territories \- your focus is continuous improvement: securing and simplifying the estate while keeping it current, resilient, and well documented.

● Maintain the infrastructure source of truth (DCIM/IPAM) so the estate is accurately documented.

● Ensure redundancy across systems and continuous monitoring of critical services.

● Produce and maintain design and systems documentation, runbooks, and recovery procedures.

● Continuously improve operational practices, processes, and infrastructure.

● Participate in an after\-hours and weekend support rotation shared across the infrastructure team.

Required Skills

● Excellent Linux system administration (Ubuntu, CentOS/RHEL) and associated technologies.

● Strong Linux command\-line proficiency and a genuine patching / currency discipline.

● Working knowledge of network services (NFS, iSCSI, SMB/Samba, LDAP, DNS).

● Strong understanding of the OSI model and networking fundamentals.

● Working knowledge of package management (rpm, deb).

● Basic version control familiarity (Git).

Required Experience

● Configuring and maintaining virtualised environments (Proxmox, VMware, or Xen).

● Storage administration with real depth \- pool design, replication, and recovery (ZFS a strong plus).

● High\-availability or load\-balancing environments \- implementing and operating them.

● Relational databases (PostgreSQL, MySQL/MariaDB) and HA clustering concepts.

● Backup and recovery tooling (Veeam, Bacula, or similar).

● Monitoring tooling (Zabbix, Prometheus/Grafana, Nagios, or similar).

● Scripting for automation (Bash, Python).

● Working within agile delivery (Jira, Bitbucket) and a change\-control discipline.

Advantageous Experience

● Cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, GCP) \- relevant to our international expansion.

● Identity and access management platforms (Authentik, Keycloak, WSO2\).

● Networking depth and troubleshooting (TCP/IP, DNS, VPN, routing/switching, firewalls).

● Server hardening and defensive systems (file integrity, IDS/IPS, Fail2Ban, auditing).

● PKI and certificate lifecycle management (Let's Encrypt, commercial CAs).

● Security audits and standards exposure (PCI\-DSS, ISMS, ISO 27000\) \- important in our regulated industry.

● Configuration management (Ansible).

Attributes

● Leadership that comes from experience; able to set and execute direction in your area.

● Self\-motivated, proactive, and comfortable working independently with little supervision.

● Strong team spirit and able to collaborate across technical and non\-technical staff.

● Logical, objective, and able to think beyond the obvious solution.

● Comfortable working under pressure and juggling multiple initiatives.

● Clear written and verbal communication, up and down the organisation.

Qualifications

● Degree/Diploma in Information Systems or related field.

● At least 5 years in IT operations and/or systems administration.

● At least 2 years in a senior or lead capacity.

How Vacancies Work

Once we receive your resume, our HR team will review it shortly. If you reach the interview stage, you'll hear from us by email or phone within two weeks. Due to application volumes we're unable to give feedback to every applicant \- but keep an eye out, as new opportunities are posted here regularly.

title: Junior Sales Manager / Engineer
location: Blackheath, WC, ZA
description:
**Position Overview**

We are seeking a dynamic, technically minded, and commercially driven Junior Sales Manager / Engineer to join our plastic manufacturing and packaging team in Cape Town. If you have a background in engineering, a technical eye, and a genuine passion for sales, marketing, and data\-driven relationship building, this role offers an exceptional career trajectory.

Positioned as a crucial bridge between the engineering world and our commercial operations, you will initially focus on learning the ropes of technical sales, customer account management, and digital marketing. With mentorship from our senior leadership, this position is structured for rapid professional development, allowing you to gradually take full ownership of strategic account management, business development, and cross\-functional product launches.

**Key Responsibilities (Growing into the Role)**

**Technical Sales, Relationship Management \& Travel**

* **Proactive Client Travel:** Regularly travel to visit both existing customer accounts to nurture relationships and new prospective leads to unlock fresh business opportunities.
* **Dedicated After\-Sales Support:** Take hands\-on ownership of comprehensive after\-sales support, serving as the dependable point of contact for clients post\-delivery to ensure long\-term satisfaction and account retention.
* **Technical Consultation:** Conduct customer site visits to understand product applications, engineering specifications, and initial manufacturing feasibility.
* **Commercial Proposals:** Assist in preparing and presenting technical quotations, commercial proposals, and supply agreements.
* **Project Management:** Take full ownership of executing smaller internal projects and growth initiatives from start to finish, managing timelines, resources, and deliverables to support business expansion.

**Engineering, Shop\-Floor Integration \& New Developments**

* **On\-the\-Ground Learning:** Integrate and interact regularly with the production and projects teams to deeply understand our specific manufacturing processes, tooling capabilities, and technical details.
* **Managing New Developments:** Lead and coordinate new product developments from a commercial standpoint, working closely with the projects team for technical assistance to get complex products that require extensive marketing and sales support successfully off the ground.
* **Cross\-Functional Liaison:** Act as the primary bridge between the customer and our internal production, tooling, procurement, and quality teams to ensure technical and commercial requirements are achieved.

**Data Representation, Forecasting \& Process Improvement**

* **Data Visualization \& Reporting:** Utilize tools like PowerBI and other advanced data\-representation methods to create clear sales forecasts, track key performance indicators (KPIs), and present data effectively to management.
* **Cross\-Functional Project Support:** Collaborate actively with the internal projects team to manage aspects of finance and procurement related to new account rollouts and product lines.
* **Systems \& Data Optimization:** Work closely with the personnel responsible for capturing data on the floor to optimize accuracy, streamline data\-capturing workflows, and continuously improve internal reporting processes.

**Marketing \& Digital Branding**

* **Brand Execution:** Take a proactive role in supporting the company's digital marketing, social media presence, and online brand positioning.
* **Content Creation:** Assist in maintaining the company website and developing marketing collateral, promotions, and campaigns alongside internal resources or external partners to drive product awareness.
* **Digital Campaigns \& Lead Generation:** Monitor and optimize incoming lead generation. Prior experience with Google Ads, SEO, and other modern digital marketing techniques to capture scalable market interest is highly advantageous.

**Key Skills \& Qualifications**

**Education, Experience \& Technical Foundation**

* **Experience:** 2 to 4 years of relevant work experience in a technical sales, engineering support, or production environment where you have been exposed to commercial operations.
* **Qualification:** A Diploma or Bachelor's Degree in Mechanical Engineering, Mechatronics, Industrial Engineering, or a related technical field.
* **Technical Acumen:** Ability to interpret technical drawings, understand physical product specifications, and translate technical needs into viable business solutions.
* **Industry Interest:** A keen interest or basic exposure to plastics manufacturing processes (e.g., injection moulding, blow moulding, extrusion and packaging) or general production environments is highly advantageous.
* **Project Management Knowledge:** Strong foundational knowledge of project management methodologies to independently plan, track, and lead smaller projects and organizational initiatives. Prior training or certification in project management is highly advantageous.

**Commercial, Sales \& Analytical Skills**

* **Sales Passion \& Modern Methodologies:** A distinct drive for business growth, relationship\-building, and commercial negotiation. As a core part of your professional development, you will be expected to know, utilize, and expand your skills with frameworks such as:
* **SPIN Selling \& Consultative/Solution Selling:** Shifting focus to active listening, diagnosing production problems, and aligning capabilities with customer needs.
* **Lead Qualification (BANT Framework):** Evaluating prospects based on Budget, Authority, Need, and Timeline.
* **Strategic Frameworks:** Building trust and navigating complex B2B cycles using concepts from Challenger, Sandler, or SNAP selling.
* **Sales Funnel Tracking:** Managing explicit steps from initial touchpoint to final order closure.
* **Data \& Analytics Literacy (PowerBI):** Solid proficiency in data representation, sales forecasting, and tracking KPIs. Hands\-on familiarity with PowerBI, advanced Excel analytics, or similar Business Intelligence (BI) platforms is strongly required.
* **Project Management Mindset:** Strong organizational skills to manage complex product rollouts. Prior training or certification in project management methodologies is highly advantageous.
* **Communication \& People Skills:** Exceptional interpersonal and communication skills. Must be a natural "people person" who excels at building rapport, active listening, and managing cross\-departmental relationships confidently.
* **Problem\-Solving Ability:** A strong technical and commercial problem solver who can think on their feet, troubleshoot customer roadblocks, and find practical manufacturing solutions.

**Language Requirements**

* **English:** Excellent verbal and written English proficiency is essential for professional correspondence, proposal writing, and technical presentations.
* **Afrikaans:** The ability to speak and understand Afrikaans is highly advantageous for regional customer engagement and shop\-floor collaboration.

**Personal Attributes**

* High learning agility with a desire to be mentored and "taught the ropes."
* Proactive, self\-motivated, resilient, and target\-driven.
* Highly collaborative with the ability to fit into a close\-knit, fast\-paced manufacturing team.
* Exceptional attention to detail, accountability, and professional integrity.

**Compensation \& Benefits**

* Competitive foundational salary structure aligned with a junior/growth\-track role.
* Retirement Annuity (RA) contribution included as part of the company benefits package.
* Performance\-based incentive structure as you begin bringing in and securing accounts.
* Clear career development pathway to evolve into a fully\-fledged Sales Engineer / Business Development Manager.
* Direct mentorship and training from senior management, Engineering Managers, and the Managing Director.

Education:

* High School (matric) (Required)

Work Location: In person

title: Technical Lead

description:
**Job Profile**

The purpose of the job is to provide a bridge between technical specialist to managerial functions within the Call Centre. The Technical Lead will draw on accumulated expertise and experience and apply this to the management of ticket\-flow and quality and act as 2IC to the Team Leader. The Technical Leader engages with Systems Technicians and Systems Engineers to resolve complex problems, he/she also acts as an escalation point for requests beyond the Systems Technician and Systems Engineers scope.

· Foster a collaborative and productive team environment.

· At specified intervals compare customer call\-records against Stowe\-records and escalate/resolve discrepancies

· Adjust and update diagnostic guides ensuring changes are landed with the team.

· Audit diagnostics attempts and tickets ensuring adherence to internal ticket logging protocol.

· Present updates and service level discussion

· Degree/ Diploma in IT or equivalent

· Minimum of 3\-5 years of experience as a Systems Engineer or related positions in call canters, customer service, or IT.

· Proficiency in troubleshooting techniques.

The **Network Engineer** is responsible for the design, implementation, support, monitoring, security, and continuous improvement of Hungry Lion's network infrastructure across all operating countries. The role ensures reliable connectivity between stores, head office, cloud platforms, data centres, and third\-party service providers, supporting business\-critical systems including POS, payments, CCTV, ERP, and collaboration platforms.  

  

The role will be accountable for maintaining high network availability, driving network standardisation across Africa, and supporting new store openings, acquisitions, and expansion projects.  

  

Travel to stores, distribution centres, and regional offices is required.  

  

**Key Responsibilities**  

  

**Network Operations \& Support**

* Manage and maintain WAN, LAN, WLAN, SD\-WAN, VPN, and internet connectivity across all Hungry Lion locations.
* Monitor network performance, availability, bandwidth utilisation, and service quality.
* Troubleshoot and resolve network incidents and escalations within agreed SLAs.
* Coordinate with service providers for fault resolution and service delivery.
* Maintain network uptime targets and minimise business disruption.
* Support after\-hours and critical incident response when required.

**Infrastructure Design \& Engineering**

* Design scalable and resilient network architectures for stores, distribution centres, and corporate offices.
* Implement network solutions aligned to business growth and expansion plans.
* Standardise network deployments across multiple African countries.
* Evaluate emerging technologies and recommend improvements.
* Support cloud connectivity requirements for Google Cloud and SaaS platforms.

**Store Connectivity \& Expansion**

* Design and maintain store network standards.
* Support all new store openings, relocations, refurbishments, and acquisitions.
* Ensure network readiness prior to store go\-live.
* Implement dual\-link and failover strategies for business continuity.
* Manage LTE backup solutions and SD\-WAN deployments.

**Security \& Compliance**

* Implement and maintain network security controls.
* Manage firewalls, VPNs, access control policies, and segmentation.
* Support cybersecurity initiatives and vulnerability remediation.
* Ensure compliance with company security policies and standards.
* Participate in audits and security assessments.

**Vendor \& Service Provider Management**

* Manage telecommunications and network service providers.
* Review provider performance against SLAs.
* Assist with contract evaluations and technical assessments.
* Drive service improvements and cost optimisation initiatives.
* Escalate chronic service issues and manage remediation plans.

**Monitoring \& Documentation**

* Maintain accurate network diagrams and documentation.
* Ensure all network assets are recorded and tracked.
* Produce operational and executive reports.
* Develop and maintain network standards and procedures.
* Maintain disaster recovery and failover documentation.

**Projects \& Continuous Improvement**

* Participate in infrastructure and digital transformation projects.
* Support POS, payment, ERP, CCTV, and cloud initiatives.
* Identify opportunities to improve reliability, performance, and cost efficiency.
* Assist in budgeting and forecasting of network\-related expenditure.
* Lead network\-related project implementations.

 **Technical Skills \& Experience**  

  

**Essential**

* 5\+ years' experience in network engineering and support.
* Experience supporting multi\-site retail environments.
* Strong understanding of:
* TCP/IP
* Routing \& Switching
* VLANs
* VPN Technologies
* Wireless Networks
* SD\-WAN
* Network Security
* DNS \& DHCP
* Firewalls
* MPLS and Broadband Services
* LTE/5G Connectivity
* Experience managing service providers and ISPs.
* Strong troubleshooting and diagnostic skills.
* Experience with network monitoring platforms.

**Preferred**

* Experience supporting African operations and remote locations.
* Experience with:
* Fortinet
* Cisco
* Aruba
* Ubiquiti
* Meraki
* Mikrotik
* Google Cloud networking
* Experience with PCI\-DSS environments.
* Experience supporting payment systems and retail POS networks.

 **Qualifications**  

  

**Essential**

* Diploma or Degree in Information Technology, Computer Science, Network Engineering, or related field.
* Preferred Certifications
* Cisco CCNA / CCNP
* Fortinet NSE
* Aruba Certified Professional
* ITIL Foundation
* Network Security Certifications

 **Key Competencies**  

  

**Technical Competencies**

* Network Architecture
* Network Security
* Performance Optimisation
* Incident Management
* Problem Solving
* Technical Documentation
* Vendor Management

**Behavioural Competencies**

* Strong ownership and accountability
* Excellent communication skills
* Customer\-focused mindset
* Attention to detail
* Ability to work under pressure
* Strong analytical thinking
* Team collaboration
* Continuous improvement mindset

 **Success Measures**


Within the first 12 months, the successful candidate should:* Establish complete visibility of all store connectivity across Africa.
* Standardise network deployments and documentation.
* Improve network uptime and resilience.
* Reduce recurring connectivity issues.
* Support the successful delivery of all new store openings.
* Implement proactive monitoring and reporting.
* Drive measurable network cost optimisation initiatives.

  

**AN INTRODUCTION TO HUNGRY LION**  

  

Hungry Lion’s journey began with a single store in Stellenbosch, Cape Town, in 1997, with a simple vision: to serve great food with genuine warmth and energy. Over the years, that vision has grown into a powerful business with a strong and expanding footprint, with hundreds of restaurants across multiple African countries.  

  

We’re growing fast and building for the future. Our ambitious expansion strategy is opening doors to new countries, new communities, and new possibilities for our teams. This means real career growth, exposure to a dynamic multinational business, and the chance to be part of a brand that’s making a powerful impact in the quick\-service restaurant (QSR) industry.  

  

Our success is powered by people; passionate, driven individuals who bring energy, creativity, and excellence to everything they do. From our restaurant teams to our corporate leadership, every person plays a vital role in delivering exceptional service and growing our brand. Joining Hungry Lion means being part of a fast\-paced, dynamic, and supportive environment where your ideas, skills, and ambition can make a real impact.

title: Junior Business Optimisation Engineer
location: Milnerton, WC, ZA
description:
Do you want to be part of a dynamic team constantly challenging the status quo and finding "smarter" ways of doing things? If you answered yes, keep reading.

 **Who Are We?**



1Nebula is a next\-generation SaaS Technology Business focused on providing businesses with cloud \& technology expense management services and tools to accelerate their cloud journey.



We hire amazing people from across South Africa, making our diverse group of team members, called N48Xers, a force to be reckoned with.

  

Learn more about our people brand by watching our culture videos:


* https://www.youtube.com/watch?v\=4JkUpUigUdM\&t\=1s
* https://www.youtube.com/watch?v\=iVk3\_vVyrW0

  


**Job Opportunity** **at 1Nebula****:**



As a Junior Business Optimisation Engineer you will create business value by applying process engineering, data visualization, reporting, and data management disciplines to design and build automated solutions that enable data\-driven decisions and optimised business processes. You will play a pivotal role in identifying opportunities for improvement within our business operations and developing strategies to optimize processes, reduce costs, and increase overall efficiency. You will work closely with cross\-functional teams to analyze data, design and implement process improvements, and contribute to achieving our organizational goals.

 **What** **Y****ou** **will do within this role (Your key responsibilities)****:**

  

* **Process Analysis:** Conduct in\-depth analysis of existing business processes to identify bottlenecks, inefficiencies, and areas for improvement.
* **Process Mapping:** Create accurate, comprehensive, and up\-to\-date process maps that serve as essential tools for process improvement, standardization, and communication within the organization.
* **Data Collection and Analysis:** Gather and analyze data from various sources to gain insights into business processes.
* **Modeling and Simulation:** Develop mathematical models and simulations to assess the impact of proposed process changes and optimisation strategies.
* **Recommendations:** Provide data\-driven recommendations and solutions to optimize business processes, reduce costs, and improve overall performance.
* **Cross\-functional Collaboration:** Collaborate with different departments to implement process improvements and ensure alignment with organizational objectives.
* **Continuous Improvement:** Continuously monitor the effectiveness of implemented changes and make adjustments as needed to drive ongoing improvement.
* **Technology Integration:** Evaluate and recommend technology solutions and tools that can enhance business processes and support optimisation efforts.
* **Documentation:** Create and maintain detailed documentation of process changes, optimisation strategies, and results.

  


**More about** **Y****ou (****O****ur requirements for the role):**



Although we believe in the potential of others more than anything else there are some minimum requirements we would look for so consider these before sending us your application

  

* Bachelor’s Degree in Industrial Engineering
* Basic knowledge of Microsoft Power Apps, Power Automate \& Power BI would be beneficial
* Excellent communication skills
* Driven and self\-motivated
* An inquisitive mind ready to take up any challenge

  


**Our** **A****mazing** **P****erks!**


* Enjoy 20 Leave days a year plus a “mulligan day” each quarter after meeting all your deliverables. That’s 4 extra leave days annually!
* High spec laptops and equipment for you to comfortably work remotely.
* A once off at home office allowance to set up your home office.
* Flexible working hours. We work on a flexible schedule from Monday to Friday where you can make the hours work for you.
* Access to our Well\-being program and Employee support services – we believe in work/life balance being of utmost importance for you and your family so this perk is for them too.
* Grow your skills and learn something new with paid for Microsoft courses and certifications.
* Take up a hobby and unlock a training allowance every 2 years to do something non\-work related.
* Give back by participating in our Culture and Social Responsibility initiatives.

title: Legal Operations Manager
location: Cape Town, WC, ZA
description:
**About us:**

  



We believe the next generation of financial services will be powered by stablecoins.



Founded in 2021, our mission at BVNK is to accelerate global money movement.



We're building stablecoin\-native infrastructure so businesses can move value instantly across borders and networks. With global licenses and T1 bank partnerships, we facilitate billions in transactions for enterprise clients like Worldpay, Deel, LianLian Global, and Rapyd.



Our technology is transforming how businesses operate globally by eliminating payment delays, reducing costs, and unlocking trapped capital.



We're a diverse team spread across EMEA, North America, and APAC, with a shared belief that stablecoins are the new infrastructure layer for financial services, and that BVNK is at the forefront of this shift.



In 2025, we secured strategic investment from Visa, following our $50 million Series B with Haun Ventures, Coinbase Ventures, Scribble Ventures, DRW Venture Capital, Avenir, and Tiger Global. With the backing of these leading investors, we're accelerating our growth – and we're looking for smart, ambitious people to help us build the future of financial services.



We're incredibly honored to have made Newsweek's list of The Top 100 Global Most Loved Workplaces three years running (most recently in 2025\) and to have been recognised by LinkedIn as one of the Top 20 Startups in the UK in 2024\.


**About this role in the team:**



We're looking for a Legal operations manager to help introduce structure, systems, and strategy into the day\-to\-day work of our legal function to free up our lawyers to focus on high\-value work and to help the team deliver faster, smarter, and with less stress.



We're looking for someone who is tech\-savvy who can both build and execute. You'll be hands\-on with legal tooling, processes, and reporting, while also collaborating with our product, engineering, and data teams to scale legal infrastructure. This is a high\-impact role at the intersection of legal, technology, and operations in a fast\-growing global fintech.


**Key Areas of Responsibilities:**


* Tooling \& Workflow Development: Build, configure, and maintain day\-to\-day tools (e.g., LawVu, Salesforce, Jira, Slack) and workflows to support legal operations. Partner with engineers and product teams to design scalable systems.
* Contract Management: Own the end\-to\-end lifecycle of contracts, including templates, versioning, automation, alerts, and integration with other business systems.
* Data \& Reporting: Create dashboards, track legal KPIs, and maintain legal data integrity across platforms. Use insights to drive team efficiency and prioritisation.
* Vendor \& Spend Management: Manage external counsel relationships, track legal spend, oversee invoice processing, and support budgeting and forecasting.
* Project Management: Lead key legal initiatives, such as licensing applications and operationalisations, tool integrations, audits, and regulatory readiness. Own timelines, risks, and delivery outcomes.
* Privacy \& Compliance Support: Collaborate with the DPO to operationalise data protection processes and support regulatory compliance.
* AI \& Automation: Identify and implement AI or automation opportunities (e.g., contract analysis, workflow routing, doc generation) to reduce manual effort.
* Training \& Enablement: Train legal and business teams on tooling and processes; promote self\-service and adoption of scalable systems.


**What we need from you:**


* Legal operations or project/program management experience in a fast\-paced legal, compliance or similar team.
* Comfortable building workflows directly in tools like Salesforce, LawVu or similar (not just overseeing implementation).
* Experience building out automations, AI programmes and workflow optimisations
* Strong technical fluency: can speak the language of engineers, draft requirements, and test configurations.
* Highly organised with experience managing complex, cross\-functional projects.
* Strong interpersonal skills with the ability to influence and partner across legal, engineering, product, and commercial teams.
* Proficient in tools like Slack, Jira, Google Workspace, DocuSign, and data reporting tools (e.g. Quicksite).


**Preferred Qualifications**


* Experience in fintech, SaaS, crypto, or other regulated tech sectors.
* Familiarity with financial services agreements or SaaS agreement structures and privacy regulations (e.g., GDPR).
* Interest in or experience implementing legal tech, CLM platforms, or workflow automation.
* A growth mindset and curiosity about building things from scratch.


**What you can expect from us:**


* Fair and **competitive salary** at every stage of your growth
* **Meaningful ownership** in the business through our employee option scheme
* **Flexible working** hours, with hybrid working at its heart
* A **culture** built on passionate, growth\-minded people
* A **flexible approach to holiday**
* Opportunities to travel to our **offices around the world**
* An **open and creative environment** where you can help us define the future of BVNK, its culture, and its opportunity sets


*At BVNK, we are focused on building a diverse and inclusive team. While you may not meet all of our requirements, we'd encourage you to apply if you meet the majority of our expectations. You may be a great fit for this role or another role in our team.*

title: 1.5 Line Engineer (24-hour rotational shift)
location: Cape Town, WC, ZA
description:
**About the Role:**  
We are seeking a motivated and skilled Combined NOC (Network Operations Centre) and SOC (Security Operations Centre) Agent to join our team. This unique hybrid role is ideal for a candidate with a strong background in IT networking and cybersecurity, who thrives in dynamic and fast\-paced environments. As a Combined NOC and SOC Agent, you will play a critical role in monitoring, managing, and securing our clients' IT infrastructure.

**Key Responsibilities:**

Network Operations Centre (NOC) Responsibilities:

* Monitor client networks, servers, and systems to ensure uptime and performance.
* Respond to alerts, investigate incidents, and troubleshoot issues to resolution.
* Perform routine maintenance tasks, including patch updates, system optimizations, and backups.
* Collaborate with technical teams to escalate and resolve advanced technical issues.
* Generate and maintain accurate system documentation and reports.

Security Operations Centre (SOC) Responsibilities:

* Monitor security tools and systems to detect and respond to potential threats or breaches.
* Analyze logs and data to identify vulnerabilities or suspicious activities.
* Respond to security incidents, including containment, mitigation, and reporting.
* Assist in implementing and managing security protocols, firewalls, and antivirus solutions.
* Participate in vulnerability assessments and provide recommendations for security improvements.

**Requirements:**

* Proven experience in IT networking, system administration, or cybersecurity.
* Strong knowledge of network protocols, routing, and switching (Cisco, Juniper, or similar).
* Experience with security tools such as SIEM, IDS/IPS, endpoint detection, and firewalls.
* Proficiency in troubleshooting Windows and Linux server environments.
* Familiarity with ITIL processes and best practices (certification is a plus).
* Excellent analytical skills with strong attention to detail.
* Ability to work in a 24/7 environment, including shift rotations as required.
* Outstanding communication skills and a customer\-focused mindset.

**Desirable Qualifications:**

* Relevant certifications such as CCNA, CCNP, CompTIA Security\+, or CISSP.
* Experience with virtualization technologies like VMware or Hyper\-V.
* Knowledge of cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, or Google Cloud).

title: Product Manager

description:
Just us at Rank Interactive
  
  

Rank Interactive, the digital arm of The Rank Group, stands as a leading global digital gaming company fuelled by top\-tier talent and technology. Recognised through iconic brands, our products and our people, we bring entertainment to millions of people worldwide on various devices daily.
  
  

Operating with a multi\-branded strategy, we offer online entertainment, bingo and slot gaming, along with a social gaming mobile experience, using a mix of proprietary and licensed software.
  
  

With offices in the UK, Gibraltar, Mauritius and South Africa while being part of a Rank Group with approximately 8,000 employees globally; we unite people and entertainment across diverse digital channels.
  
  

Joining our high\-performing and inclusive culture, you'll instantly become part of a team supporting our customer\-facing initiatives.
  
  

We're looking for a Product Manager who will be responsible for defining, delivering, and optimising products and services that meet customer needs and support business objectives. Working closely with Design, Engineering, and key stakeholders. You will own problem spaces, translate strategy into actionable plans, and ensure value is delivered through well\-prioritised, evidence\-led product decisions. You will contribute across the full product lifecycle from discovery to delivery and optimisation balancing customer value, commercial outcomes, and technical feasibility.
  
  

**Responsibilities and Accountabilities:**  

* Own and manage clearly defined product areas, translating strategy and objectives into well\-scoped initiatives and outcomes.
* Collaborate with Experience Designers and Engineers throughout discovery, delivery, and optimisation to ensure solutions solve real customer problems.
* Define and prioritise product requirements, balancing customer needs, business value, and delivery constraints.
* Use customer insight, market research, and performance data to inform decisions and iterate on solutions.
* Manage backlogs, roadmaps, and delivery plans to ensure timely, high\-quality execution.
* Engage with stakeholders to share progress, manage expectations, and surface risks or trade\-offs.
* Measure outcomes and benefits, using in\-market results to continuously improve product performance.
* Contribute positively to team standards, ways of working, and continuous improvement.


**Qualifications:**  

* Experience managing digital products or services through the full product lifecycle.
* Solid understanding of product management principles, including discovery, prioritisation, and delivery.
* Ability to analyse data and customer insight to support decision\-making.
* Strong collaboration skills and comfort working in cross\-functional teams.
* Clear communication skills, with the ability to explain decisions and trade\-offs.
* A proactive mindset with curiosity to learn, improve, and grow product craft.

title: Engineering Manager
location: Cape Town, WC, ZA
description:
At 2U, we are all in on purpose. We are motivated by our mission – to eliminate the back row in education – and connected by our shared passion to deliver world\-class digital education at scale. As the parent company of edX, the world's leading online learning platform, 2U powers more than 4,000 online higher education offerings – from free courses to full degrees. Together with more than 230 colleges, universities, and corporate partners, we are helping to unlock human potential.


**What We're Looking For:**



An Engineering Manager to lead and manage operations within the Salesforce and CRM domain, ensuring seamless coordination of people, costs, and platform integrity. You will drive business outcomes by influencing technical roadmaps that leverage the full Salesforce ecosystem, with a strategic focus on Agentforce, Data Cloud and AI\-driven automation. You are expected to navigate ambiguity and translate business priorities into high\-performing, scalable CRM solutions.


**Responsibilities Include, But Are Not Limited To:**


**Technical**


* **Architectural Ownership:** Own the production environment and maintain a robust Salesforce architecture, ensuring scalability across Sales, Service, and Experience Clouds.
* **Data Strategy \& Orchestration:** Lead the implementation and management of Salesforce Data Cloud, ensuring high\-quality data ingestion, harmonization, and unification (Identity Resolution) to power downstream AI and analytics.
* **AI \& Agentic Workflows:** Lead the strategy and deployment of Agentforce, building and managing autonomous agents that leverage Data Cloud insights to streamline business processes.
* **Requirements Alignment:** Collaborate with Product and Stakeholders to ensure CRM and Data strategies align with technical capabilities and long\-term roadmap goals.
* **SDLC \& Data Governance:** Accountable for the sprint lifecycle, ensuring best practices in Salesforce DevOps and strict adherence to data privacy and governance standards within the CRM.
* **Quality \& Integrity:** Ensure code integrity (Apex, LWC) and declarative configuration quality, proactively seeking platform optimizations to reduce technical debt and data silos.

  


**People Management**


* **Performance Leadership:** Take hands\-on responsibility for the CRM engineering team's performance and output.
* **Mentorship:** Set S.M.A.R.T goals, providing specific guidance on Salesforce career paths and emerging AI technologies and Data Cloud.
* **Talent Pipeline:** Build and grow the team through recruitment and interviews, specifically looking for talent capable of navigating the shift toward agentic AI.
* **Team Health:** Prioritize employee morale, fostering an environment of continuous learning and high engagement.

  


**Accountability for Results**


* **Strategic Execution:** Collaborate on strategic requirements, translating high\-level business needs into operational Salesforce execution plans.
* **SME Leadership:** Act as the CRM Subject Matter Expert, identifying potential platform bottlenecks and solving complex integration problems.
* **Resource Allocation:** Own sprint planning and resource allocation to ensure high\-value features and Agentforce workflows are delivered on time.
* **Domain Representation:** Represent the CRM domain in executive meetings, influencing decisions with data\-driven insights.

  


**Leadership and Personal Development**


* **Culture Carrier:** Embody company principles and promote an inclusive, supportive, and high\-performance work environment.
* **Continuous Learning:** Stay at the forefront of the Salesforce release cycle, specifically regarding AI, Data Cloud, and Agentforce innovations.
* **Communication:** Enhance transparency between the technical team and non\-technical business units.

  


**Management Responsibilities**


* Day\-to\-day oversight of CRM systems, and third\-party integrations within your remit.
* Direct management of Salesforce Engineers, Admins, and Declarative Developers.

  


**Things That Should Be In Your Background:**


* **Experience:** 7–10 years of software engineering experience, with at least 3–5 years focused on the Salesforce ecosystem and 2\+ years in a supervisory or management role.
* **Agentforce Mastery:** Proven experience or deep theoretical knowledge in deploying Salesforce Agentforce (or similar LLM\-based agentic workflows).
* **Education:** Degree in Computer Science, Software Development, or a related field.
* **Certifications:** Salesforce Certified Administrator, Salesforce Data Cloud Consultant or Platform Developer II is preferred; Architecture certifications are a major plus.
* **Business Acumen:** Strong ability to balance business urgency with technical sustainability.

  


**Benefits \& Culture**



Our global employee base is a diverse collection of innovators, dreamers, and doers working together to transform lives through higher education. We believe that every employee can advance our shared purpose, and that life at 2U should be fun and meaningful. If you're excited by the opportunity to provide over 40 million learners and counting with access to world\-class online higher education, then join us – and do work that makes a difference. \#NoBackRow



We offer comprehensive benefits (unique per country) and excellent work/life balance.  

Full\-time, ZA benefits include:


* 2 complimentary Getsmarter short courses per year
* Subsidised medical aid with Discovery Health Medical Scheme
* 4% 2U contribution towards Discovery Life Pension Fund and Group Risk Benefit
* Employee Assistance Program (EAP)
* Generous leave policy including time off to volunteer for non\-profit organization, study leave, sports leave and a company\-wide festive season break


**2U Diversity and Inclusion Statement**

  


At 2U, we are committed to building and sustaining a culture of belonging, respect, and inclusion. We are proud of the steps we've taken to bring together an employee base that embodies diverse walks of life, ideas, genders, ages, races, cultures, sexual orientations, abilities and other unique qualities. We strive to offer a workplace where every employee feels empowered by what makes us different, as well as by how we are alike.



2U is committed to providing reasonable accommodations during our recruitment process. If you need assistance or accommodations, please reach out to us at: recruitingaccommodations@2u.com.
  

  

**About edX**



edX is the education movement for restless learners and a leading global online learning platform from 2U, Inc. (Nasdaq: TWOU). Together with the majority of the world's top\-ranked universities and industry\-leading companies, we bring our community of over 45 million learners world\-class education to support them at every stage of their lives and careers, from free courses to full degrees. And we're not stopping there — we're relentlessly pursuing our vision of a world where every learner can access education to unlock their potential, without the barriers of cost or location. Learn more at edX.org.



Learn more at https://2u.com/careers/

All employees may be required to perform duties outside of their normal responsibilities from time to time, as needed.

title: Senior Software Engineer
location: Claremont, WC, ZA
description:
**About the Role**

  

Kurtosys builds the platform that asset and wealth managers use to produce and distribute client\-facing reports, marketing materials, and data\-driven documents at scale. We're a regulated fintech \- ISO\-27001 certified, operating across four global offices, fully hosted on AWS. The engineering team is small, senior, and moving fast.

  



This role sits in Product Engineering. You'll own features end\-to\-end: from understanding the business problem, through architecture and implementation, to deployment and production monitoring. The work spans front\-end (React, TypeScript), back\-end services (Node.js, serverless), databases (PostgreSQL, SingleStore), and cloud infrastructure (AWS, Cloudflare). You'll work across all of it \- not because we expect you to be an expert in everything on day one, but because we expect you to be capable of working wherever the problem takes you.

  



What makes this role different from the other senior engineering listings you'll see this week: AI is not a line item on our roadmap. It's how we work. The engineering team uses AI daily \- for architecture, implementation, code review, testing, and delivery. We have AI agents embedded in our workflows, shared prompting standards across the codebase, and an expectation that every engineer uses these tools to move faster and think more clearly.

  



We're not looking for someone who is "willing to embrace AI." We're looking for someone who already has, and who can show us something we haven't tried yet.

  


**What You'll Actually Do**

* Solve business problems, not just technical ones. You'll work with product managers and designers to understand what clients need, then figure out the right way to build it. The best engineers here challenge requirements, not just implement them.



\- Own features from architecture to production. You'll design APIs, model data, build UIs, write tests, deploy, and monitor. You won't hand things off to another team \- you'll see them through.


* Ship with AI as your primary tool. You'll use AI coding tools to prototype, iterate, and deliver at a pace that wouldn't be possible without them. You'll also know when AI is wrong and have the judgement to catch it.


* Improve how we work, not just what we build. We value engineers who bring ideas about process, tooling, and standards. If you've found a better way to do something, we expect you to advocate for it.


* Work in a regulated environment. Our clients are in financial services. That means accessibility compliance (WCAG 2\.2 AA), security best practices, data isolation, and audit trails are not optional. You'll need to build with these constraints as defaults, not afterthoughts.

  


**What We're Looking For**

  



Judgement and problem\-solving ability. You can break down an ambiguous problem, identify what matters, make a decision, and explain why. You don't wait to be told what to build \- you figure it out and propose a path.

  



An embedded AI workflow. You don't just "use Copilot sometimes." You have a workflow \- prompting patterns, tool configurations, an understanding of where AI helps and where it hallucinates. You can describe a specific task you completed with AI this month, what went wrong, and how you fixed it.

  



Specificity over generality. When we ask about your experience, you name the system, the client, the decision, and the trade\-off. You don't describe what a good engineer would do in theory \- you tell us what you actually did.

  



Communication that influences. You can explain a technical decision to a non\-technical stakeholder. You can write a proposal that gets buy\-in. You can disagree with a colleague constructively. In a remote\-heavy, async team, this matters as much as code quality.

  



Full\-stack capability. You're comfortable in React and TypeScript on the front end, Node.js on the back end, SQL at the data layer, and AWS for infrastructure. You don't need to be an expert in all of it, but you need to be able to work across all of it without waiting for someone else.

  



Ownership and reliability. You finish what you start. You test your own code. You monitor what you deploy. You don't consider a feature done when the PR is merged \- you consider it done when it's working in production and the client is using it.

  


**What Will Set You Apart**

  



\- You've built or contributed to something with AI that changed how your team works \- not just used it for autocomplete.


* You have opinions about software architecture and can defend them with trade\-offs, not dogma.


* You've worked in a regulated or compliance\-heavy environment and understand why constraints exist.


* You've shipped a side project, contributed to open source, or written about engineering publicly.


* You bring experience or genuine curiosity in the financial services or investment management domain.

  


**Tech Stack**

  



You'll work across these technologies. Experience with all of them is not required \- being able to learn quickly with AI assistance is.

  


* Frontend: React, TypeScript


* Backend: Node.js, TypeScript, Serverless (AWS Lambda, Cloudflare Workers)


* Data: PostgreSQL (Aurora), SingleStore, RDS MySQL


* Infrastructure: AWS (ECS, Lambda, S3, SQS), Cloudflare (CDN, Workers), Docker

  


**How We Work**

  


* Hybrid from our Cape Town office (Claremont). Flexible hours.



\- Small, senior team. You'll work directly with the CTO and senior engineers \- not through layers of management.


* AI\-first engineering. AI tools are part of our daily workflow. We share prompting standards, maintain AI agent configurations across our codebase, and expect every engineer to use these tools to multiply their output.


* Agile without the theatre. We plan, build, ship, and learn. We don't do ceremony for the sake of process.

  


**Benefits**

  



Hybrid working with flexible hours



Generous leave policy



Employee wellbeing programme



Access to learning platforms



Regular team events



Working with a global team across four offices

  

Get in touch to learn more!
